title: Spinach & Cheese Muffins
steps: Preheat the oven to 180C.
Melt the butter in a large bowl.
Add the eggs and whisk.
Add the milk and mix.
Defrost the spinach in a small bowl in the microwave, then chop it.
Grate the cheese.
Sieve the plain flour into the large bowl with the butter, eggs and milk.
Add the cheese and chopped spinach, then mix.
Grease a muffin tray with butter.
Spoon the mixture into the tray and bake for 15-16 minutes until golden.
notes: Toddler-friendly snack. Keep as recipe-only by default rather than adding to the weekly meal list.

title: Coconut Chicken Curry

ingredients.name: chicken breasts
ingredients.notes: skinless and boneless, cut into bite size pieces

steps: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven, add the chicken and season with salt and pepper.
Cook the chicken for about 5 minutes or until no longer pink.
Add the onion, garlic and curry powder, stir and cook for 2 more minutes.
Add the chicken broth, coconut milk, diced tomatoes, tomato paste and sugar, then stir everything together.
Bring to a boil, cover with a lid, turn the heat down and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve over rice.
notes: Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days, or freeze in a shallow container once fully cooled and stir well while reheating. Curry powder blends vary in heat — start with 1 tablespoon if unsure, then adjust. Mild curry powder works best for a family-friendly version.

title: Classic Omurice (Japanese chicken rice omelette)

source.label: Sirogohan.com — 基本のオムライス

ingredients.name: cooked rice (warm)
ingredients.notes: [ASSUMPTION] quantity inferred from sirogohan chicken rice recipe — not stated explicitly on omurice page

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: for finishing the chicken rice, plus a pinch earlier

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: 2 per portion, cooked one portion at a time

ingredients.name: butter for cooking eggs
ingredients.notes: [ASSUMPTION] small knob per portion — page mentions butter for the pan but exact qty not given in source snippets

steps: Dice the carrot into 4–5mm pieces, the onion into 5–7mm pieces, and the chicken thigh into roughly 1cm cubes.

Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat, then add the onion, carrot and chicken together. Stir-fry for around 3 minutes until the onion turns translucent and the chicken is cooked through.
Season with a pinch of salt and pepper. Lower the heat slightly, then add the ketchup, tomato puree and butter. Stir until the butter melts and the ketchup coats everything evenly.
Add the warm rice and mix through until the colour is uniform. Season with 1/4 tsp salt and a little pepper, taste, and adjust. Divide into 2 portions and set aside.
For each serving, crack 2 eggs into a bowl, add 2 pinches of salt, and beat hard with chopsticks, dragging along the base of the bowl until no streaks of white remain.
Melt a knob of butter in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat, pour in the egg mixture, and stir gently as it sets so the surface stays half-cooked.
Place one portion of chicken rice in the centre of the egg, fold the sides over, then tip the pan to roll it onto a plate seam-side down. Repeat for the second serving.
Make the ketchup-butter sauce: combine 4 tbsp ketchup, 1.5 tbsp water and ~10g butter in a small pan over low heat, stirring until the butter melts and the sauce is glossy.
Spoon the sauce over the omurice and serve immediately.
notes: Source page did not fully render via web fetch — ingredient quantities reconstructed from search-result snippets of the sirogohan omurice page and the linked chicken rice recipe (sirogohan.com/recipe/tikirice/). [ASSUMPTION] flags mark items where exact source quantities were not visible; rice, onion and carrot quantities are best-guess based on the related chicken rice recipe and standard 2-portion sirogohan ratios. Chicken rice can be made the day before. The ketchup-butter sauce is the signature finishing touch on this recipe.

title: Smoky & Creamy Caramelised Onion Butter Beans
steps: In a medium frying pan over medium heat, add a couple of tbsp of oil then the onions. Fry until softened and caramelised, stirring often.
Add the garlic and tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Deglaze the pan with the white wine and simmer for a couple of minutes until mostly evaporated. Skip if no wine to hand.
Add the butter beans with their liquid and the lentils.
Add the double cream and bring to a simmer for 5 minutes.
Squeeze in the lemon juice and taste for seasoning.
Top with chopped parsley and basil if using, and serve.
notes: Laura's tweaks applied: 2 cans of butter beans, lentils added, tomato purée and cream doubled. Lentils added with the beans — if using dried lentils rather than pre-cooked, add extra liquid and simmer longer until tender.

title: Japanese Curry

ingredients.name: meat (beef, chicken, pork or prawns)
ingredients.notes: brown first; skip for a vegetarian version

ingredients.name: garam masala
ingredients.notes: plus optional extra 1 tsp at the end

ingredients.name: Japanese curry cubes
ingredients.notes: e.g. S&B Golden Curry — choose mild for kid-friendly

ingredients.name: milk or extra water
ingredients.notes: to loosen if the sauce is too thick

steps: Dice the onion. In a large pan or pot, brown your chosen meat (beef, chicken, pork or prawns) with the onion. Skip the meat for a vegetarian version.
Add enough water to cover, then add the carrots and potatoes.
Add a handful of fresh tomatoes (or 1/4 tin of chopped tomatoes) and stir in 1 tsp sugar if using.
Season with salt, pepper and 1 tbsp garam masala.
Simmer with meat for 1–2 hours; for vegetable-only, simmer up to 45 minutes.
Lower the heat. Drop in the Japanese curry cubes and stir until they dissolve and the sauce is smooth.
If the sauce is too thick, loosen with a splash of milk or water.
Optionally add another 1 tsp of garam masala at the end for a stronger spice hit.
Optional: serve alongside katsu chicken, pork or prawns.
Serve over rice or udon noodles.
notes: Family recipe. Flexible — works with any protein or veg-only. Japanese curry cubes (S&B Golden Curry, House Vermont Curry, etc.) are the key flavour base; choose mild for the toddler-friendly version. Long meat simmer (1–2h) makes the meat tender; reduce to 45 minutes for veg-only.

title: Miso Soup

ingredients.name: dark miso paste
ingredients.notes: to taste — add residue at the bottom for flavour

steps: Bring water to the boil in a saucepan.
Add the dashi tea bag and let it infuse, then remove it once the water has boiled.
Stir in the dashi powder.
Add the white onion, potato and carrot. Simmer until just tender.
Add the mangetout and cook briefly.
Once everything is cooked through, turn off the heat.
Add the tofu (cubed) and wakame seaweed.
Stir in the dark miso paste — about 1 tbsp to taste — including any residue from the bottom of the tub for extra flavour.
Stir in the light miso paste — another 1 tbsp or so to taste.
Warm gently to serve. Don't boil after adding the miso — it dulls the flavour.
notes: Family recipe. Keeps well in the fridge for up to 3 days; reheat gently rather than boiling.

title: Yorkshire Puddings

ingredients.name: vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: a little, for the muffin tin

steps: Whisk the flour, eggs and milk together into a smooth batter.
Rest the batter in the fridge before cooking — it helps them rise.
Add a little oil to each cup of a muffin tin and preheat in a hot oven (220°C/200°C fan) until the oil is smoking hot.
Pour the batter into the hot oil — equal amounts per cup.
Return the tin straight back to the oven. Try not to open the door once they're in — they'll fail to rise.
Bake for 25–30 minutes until risen and golden.
notes: Family recipe. Makes 6 small or 4 large puddings. The cold batter + smoking-hot oil contrast is what gives the rise.

title: Pancakes / Crepes
steps: Whisk flour, eggs and milk together into a smooth, thin batter.
Heat a medium pan to medium-hot, lightly greased.
Ladle the batter into the pan, swirling to coat the base in a thin layer.
Fry for 30–60 seconds per side until lightly golden.
notes: Family recipe — thin French-style crepes. Sweet (lemon and sugar, Nutella) or savoury (cheese and ham). Makes 6–8 crepes.
